title: SEBI Eases Reporting Norms for Stock Brokers and Demat Accounts
content:
# SEBI Eases Reporting Norms for Stock Brokers and Demat Accounts

Ek important update hai. Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) ne stock brokers aur depository participants ke liye kuch reporting requirements mein relaxations announce kiye hain, jisse "ease of doing business" promote hoga. Yeh changes 23 March 2026 ko ek Circular ke through notify kiye gaye hain.

Yeh naye rules effective honge from April 17, 2026.

**Key Relaxations aur Changes:**

*   **Demat Account Reporting:** Ab stock brokers ko stock exchanges ko apne demat accounts report karne ki requirement nahi hogi. Instead, depositories hi sabhi demat accounts ki details (opened or closed) stock exchanges ko provide karenge.
*   **Bank Account Reporting:** Jo stock brokers banks ya primary dealers bhi hain, unhe ab sirf woh bank accounts report karne honge jo stock broking activities ke liye use hote hain, na ki unke saare bank accounts.
*   **Bank Account Reporting Timelines:** Stock brokers ko bank accounts kholne ya band karne ki information stock exchanges ko seven working days ke andar deni hogi.
*   **Demat Account Tagging Exemption:** Primary dealers jo stock brokers bhi hain, unke demat accounts jo exclusively non-broking activities ke liye use hote hain, unpar tagging ki requirement apply nahi hogi.
*   **Nomenclature:** Bank aur demat accounts ke liye uniform nomenclature (naamkaran) mandatory kiya gaya hai, aur ek baar demat account ke liye nomenclature assign hone ke baad, usse modify nahi kiya ja sakta.

Yeh amendments Master Circular for Stock Brokers dated June 17, 2025 ke Paragraph 15 mein kiye gaye hain, jiska aim compliance burden kam karna aur regulatory efficiency badhana hai.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.sebi.gov.in/legal/circulars/mar-2026/ease-of-doing-business-measures-relaxations-in-certain-reporting-requirements-for-certain-stock-brokers-and-doing-away-with-the-requirement-of-reporting-of-demat-account_76562.html)

*Published on: 25 March 2026*
